items.title: NYC native Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden:
items.author: Tom Orsborn

items.title: Keldon Johnson scored just 7 points, but Game 3 was one of his best of the playoffs. In 17 minutes, he was a team-best plus-14 and did a good job muscling up against Towns.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Keldon Johnson scored just 7 points, but Game 3 was one of his best of the playoffs. In 17 minutes, he was a team-best plus-14 and did a good job muscling up against Towns.

"I just tried to make it tough on him, make his catches tough, try to make him uncomfortable," he said.

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Brian Taylor. Princeton SG went to play w/Dr J Nets in ABA + was ROY & 2x all-star. NJ let him go in 76 + he was good w/KC, DEN, SDC thru 82. Led ABA + NBA in 3%. Retired in 82 due to Achilles tear. Underrated & would've been analytics fave.

items.title: The passive voice regarding the description of action happening in public (or even in private but witnessed first hand) in journalism needed to go away a long, long time ago.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
The passive voice regarding the description of action happening in public (or even in private but witnessed first hand) in journalism needed to go away a long, long time ago. 
If you saw it, you saw it. 
If you saw someone do it, they did it.

items.title: June 9, 2005: A Manu Ginóbili and-1 during the Spurs' 84-69 victory over Detroit in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 9, 2005: A Manu Ginóbili and-1 during the Spurs' 84-69 victory over Detroit in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Ginóbili finished with a game-high 26 PTS (10-16 FG) and 9 REB, and Tim Duncan added 24 PTS/17 REB for San Antonio. Chauncey Billups led the Pistons with 25 PTS/6 AST.

items.title: “It's hard to put your head out of the water."
items.author: Kelly Iko
items.body_excerpt:
“It's hard to put your head out of the water."

In the loudest city in the world, with the pressure of an 0-2 hole threatening their season, Victor Wembanyama did what he does best — found the silence. 

On the man in the Garden, French art and survival instincts.

sports.yahoo.com/nba/article/...

media.title: NBA Finals: Victor Wembanyama finds redemption with Madison Square Garden takeover
media.description: After taking the blame for the Spurs' Game 2 loss and series deficit, Wemby responded the only way he knows how — with cool, calm dominance.
